FFmpeg release: 8.1.2 "Hoare"
Release tag: n8.1.2
Release commit: 38b88335f99e76ed89ff3c93f877fdefce736c13
Build profile revision: 4
Deployment target: macOS 14.0
Architectures: arm64 and x86_64, built separately and merged with lipo
License target: LGPL 2.1-or-later

The two slices use the same component profile. ARCH, SDKROOT, compiler paths,
prefix, and -arch flags are supplied by Scripts/build-ffmpeg.sh.
External package discovery is disabled with the repository's fail-closed
Scripts/support/pkg-config-disabled shim.

Pinned source patch:

Vendor/FFmpeg/patches/0001-avformat-fd-accept-descriptor-in-url.patch
SHA-256: 7f2095aae417027256fa76640aa1bfe44e37cc46bdffea269e8203353930cc5d

The patch preserves the legacy `fd:` behavior and adds strict decimal
`fd:<number>` URLs for nonnegative descriptors through INT_MAX. An explicit URL
descriptor takes precedence over the `fd` protocol option. Signs, whitespace,
trailing characters, and numeric overflow are rejected. On Darwin, every
seekable `fd` context keeps an independent logical offset and uses `pread` /
`pwrite`; streamed descriptors keep the legacy sequential behavior. This avoids
shared-open-file-description offset interference when the MOV muxer reopens an
explicit `fd:<number>` URL during `+faststart`. That descriptor must be opened
for both reading and writing, and should point to an empty regular file. Because
this profile relies on Darwin's positional-I/O semantics, enabling the patched
`fd` protocol for a non-Darwin target fails at compile time.

--target-os=darwin
--enable-cross-compile
--cpu=generic
--disable-autodetect
--disable-everything
--disable-network
--disable-avdevice
--disable-ffplay
--disable-doc
--disable-debug
--disable-shared
--enable-static
--disable-x86asm
--disable-iconv
--disable-audiotoolbox
--enable-videotoolbox
--enable-ffmpeg
--enable-ffprobe
--enable-protocol=fd
--enable-protocol=file
--enable-protocol=pipe
--enable-demuxer=mov
--enable-muxer=mp4
--enable-decoder=h264
--enable-decoder=aac
--enable-parser=h264
--enable-parser=aac
--enable-encoder=h264_videotoolbox
--enable-encoder=aac
--enable-filter=scale
--enable-filter=aresample
--fatal-warnings

Explicitly absent:

--enable-gpl
--enable-version3
--enable-nonfree
--enable-libx264
--enable-libx265

No external --enable-lib* option is used. Network support and all network
protocols are disabled. Only the local fd, file, and pipe protocols are enabled.
The only bundled command-line programs are ffmpeg and ffprobe.
